source_detail: EU CRMA provisions could apply in NI if agreed by UK-EU Joint Committee - potential dual UK+EU market access for NI midstream
Curraghinalt M&I 3.79 Moz Au @8.5 g/t (+3.0 Moz inferred); ~15000 t Cu over life plus Sb/Te/Bi/Co; NI's one known polymetallic critical-mineral deposit
Belfast Harbour throughput 2024; logistics asset and offshore-wind decommissioning hub (turbine-magnet feedstock for Ionic/Plaswire)
Real strategic-reserve targets - calibrates the stockpile lever (coupling STOCKPILE_DEPTH=0.5 demand-yr ~180 days); shows the stockpile is a months-to-~2yr bridge, not a durable fix
$110/kg NdPr 10-year price-floor offtake used to de-risk a magnet-materials plant - evidence that offtake+price floor is the midstream unlock (model lever: secondary_market_support)

State-capital tools (offtake, price floors, equity stakes) de-risk price-volatile midstream where private lenders won't; one-off grants alone don't secure supply - support must be structural/recurring

source: US DOE Critical Minerals and Materials Accelerator 2025
source_detail: Public cost-share to cross the pilot->commercial 'valley of death' (20% at prototype, 50% at pilot/demonstration); the decisive barrier to business participation in midstream processing/recycling

source: US-Australia Critical Minerals Partnership Fund (Third Way 2025)
source_detail: Blended public-private finance that crowds in private capital for FOAK plants; model for an NI/UK midstream scale-up facility

source: EU Critical Raw Materials Act (European Commission 2025)
source_detail: Strategic-project permitting target: 15 mo processing/recycling, 27 mo extraction, plus finance facilitation + offtake matchmaking - a one-stop participation package NI could access via Windsor Framework Art 13(4)

Shock vector: 'increasingly concentrated processing and mining supply chains' vulnerable to 'natural disasters, war or geopolitical fallout' and 'shortages or disruption' - basis for the Q2.4 dominant-supplier export-ban shock
Two government objectives: optimise domestic production; build resilient UK & global supply networks (partnerships, diversification). Define the Q2.4 government roles
'joint action between industry and government ... in a more coordinated way' - grounds the strategic-coordinator role, which the model finds most robust
Diversification cuts effective single-country concentration by up to 45% at full lever (MFA), calibrated to Japan cutting China REE dependence ~90%->~58% (~36%), targeting <50% (~44%)

Japan reduced Chinese REE dependence from ~90% to ~58% after the 2010 export ban via coordinated stockpiling + overseas equity/offtake + recycling + substitution; validates the strategic-coordinator posture
Equity + loans for guaranteed REE supply - the diversification-via-offtake mechanism; saved Lynas and diversified Japan's supply
Stockpiles 'provide an important buffer against sudden disruptions while countries develop new diversified sources' (oil SPR precedent) - validates the bridge-not-fix design

Positive socio-economic impacts: job creation, multiplier effects, taxes & royalties, infrastructure, training/skills investment, employment of nationals/locals, in-country procurement - the Q2.6 benefit suite
30-yr dynamic I-O uses NI 2016 + Scotland 2016 Type I/II multipliers; £5m final-demand example (Table 2.2); model proxy A-matrix tuned to these anchors

Benefits can be 'economically detached from the regions'; Galmoy & Lisheen (closed Irish mines) used national/international specialist firms, limiting local employment - retained share depends on local-content + skills (Q2.5)
Effective public take on GVA for the Q2.6 tax proxy; conservative - excludes mineral royalties (gold/silver Crown-vested in NI)
All mining scenarios 'rely on access to a pool of skilled labour'; skills shortage caps realised employment benefit - the skills pipeline (Q2.5) is the gating constraint

Impact groups: land transformation (take/habitat/landscape), water availability & quality, air quality & noise, GHG, biodiversity, mine closure, tailings/waste rock; receptors are people + biodiversity; impacts site-specific
avoid (recycle/design-for-disassembly) > mitigate (high-ESG: water stewardship, progressive rehab, biodiversity net gain, dust/noise controls) > manage closure (bonded) - the impact-reduction order
Secondary/magnet recycling carbon footprint ~30% of primary mine+refine; recycling also avoids the land/water/biodiversity/tailings burden almost entirely (impact_module)
High-ESG/low-impact design cuts a primary mine's local pressures (water/land/biodiversity/waste/PM) by up to 35% in the model (impact_module.esg_mitigation)
Water/land/biodiversity/tailings relative pressure per £m activity (mining-referenced, dimensionless PROXY); replace with site EIA + ecoinvent/EXIOBASE characterisation factors
